files_examined: doc/release/next_whats_new/ (checked via GitHub — no new RST found)
summary: This PR adds BivarColorbar, MultivarColorbar, Figure.bivar_colorbar, Figure.multivar_colorbar, and pyplot.bivar_colorbar to support bivariate and multivariate color visualization, and extends imshow/pcolor/pcolormesh to accept K-channel data arrays. The implementation is substantial and largely functional, but has several critical blocking issues: (1) Colorizer.get_clim(), Colorizer.vmin, Colorizer.vmax, and Colorizer.clip silently change return types from scalars to tuples for the 1-component case, breaking all existing callers without any deprecation period; (2) no what's-new RST was added for any of the new public API; (3) Figure.multivar_colorbar references an undefined function make_multivar_axes_gridspec at runtime; (4) BivarColorbar.__init__ uses a mutable list as a default argument; (5) BivarColorbar.update_normals() has a self._draw_all() call commented out, meaning norm changes on the mappable will not update the colorbar in interactive use; (6) MultivarColorbar.remove() references self.ax which does not exist (the class stores self.axes). These must be resolved before merging.

issues.comment: Colorizer.get_clim() now returns tuple[tuple, tuple] instead of tuple[float, float] for the single-component case — it returns ((vmin,), (vmax,)) instead of (vmin, vmax). This silently breaks every existing caller of colorizer.get_clim(). The old documented return was '(min, max)'. This must go through deprecation: keep the old scalar return in 3.N, emit _api.warn_deprecated() that the return type will change in 3.N+2, and add a doc/api/next_api_changes/deprecations/ RST. The same applies to ColorizingArtist.get_clim(), which now raises AttributeError for n_components > 1, also a breaking change.

Colorizer.vmin and Colorizer.vmax property return types changed from float|None to tuple[float|None,...]|None, and Colorizer.clip changed from bool to tuple[bool,...]. These are breaking API changes for any downstream code using these properties. Users who wrote `if colorizer.vmin < 0: ...` will get a TypeError. The stub correctly documents the new behaviour, but the behaviour itself must be deprecated before shipping — keep the scalar return and emit _api.warn_deprecated() in the getter.

Figure.multivar_colorbar calls cbar.make_multivar_axes_gridspec(ax, ...) in the use_gridspec=True branch, but no such function is defined anywhere in this PR or the existing codebase. The diff defines make_multivar_axes (without 'gridspec') but never defines make_multivar_axes_gridspec. The default path (use_gridspec=True) raises AttributeError at runtime. Either define make_multivar_axes_gridspec, or fall back to make_multivar_axes unconditionally.

No what's-new RST file was added for any of the new public API: BivarColorbar, MultivarColorbar, Figure.bivar_colorbar, Figure.multivar_colorbar, pyplot.bivar_colorbar, or the K-channel imshow/pcolor/pcolormesh support. The project policy (SKILL.md §4) requires a doc/release/next_whats_new/<slug>.rst for every user-visible feature. Additionally, none of the new public classes or methods carry a '.. versionadded:: 3.N' directive in their docstrings.

BivarColorbar.__init__ uses a mutable list as a default argument: `ticklocations=['auto', 'auto']`. Python evaluates default argument values once at function-definition time, so mutations to the default object are shared across calls. Use `ticklocations=None` with `if ticklocations is None: ticklocations = ['auto', 'auto']`, or a tuple default `ticklocations=('auto', 'auto')` since the code immediately does `list(ticklocations)` anyway.

BivarColorbar.update_normals() has `#self._draw_all()` commented out. When the attached mappable emits a 'changed' callback (e.g., after autoscaling or norm change), the colorbar does not redraw. Tests pass only because they directly trigger redraws through other side effects. In interactive use, changing the norm on the mappable will silently leave the colorbar stale. Remove the comment marker and restore the call.

MultivarColorbar.remove() references self.ax, but the class stores self.axes (plural), not self.ax. This raises AttributeError whenever remove() is called. The correct reference is self.axes[0] (or the local `ax` variable already computed a few lines above).

MultivarColorbar has no class-level docstring. It is a new public API class and must have a NumPy-style docstring explaining purpose, Parameters, and usage. BivarColorbar above it has a proper docstring; MultivarColorbar has none.

After the pcolor refactor, `collection._scale_norm(norm, vmin, vmax)` is called after a Colorizer was already constructed with `colorizer = mcolorizer.Colorizer(cmap=cmap, norm=norm)`. Verify this does not double-apply vmin/vmax to the norm. The same pattern appears in pcolormesh (~line 6934). At minimum add a comment explaining why _scale_norm is called post-construction.

Large block of commented-out code (~lines 595–630: _y_limits_changed, _x_limits_changed, _update_limits methods stored in triple-quoted strings used as block comments) should not be merged. Remove it entirely or open a follow-up issue. Triple-quoted strings used as block comments also confuse documentation tools.

BivarColorbar._draw_all() calls self._image.remove() then creates a new pcolormesh on every redraw. This is fragile — if an exception is raised midway, the axes can be left without a colorbar image. The standard approach (used by Colorbar) is to update the existing collection's array/data rather than remove-and-recreate.

The error message for MultivarColormap mappables is missing a space: `"colorbar can only be together with a" "scalar colormap"` (adjacent string literals concatenate without a space between 'a' and 'scalar'). Fix: `'colorbar can only be used with a scalar colormap; please use Figure.multivar_colorbar instead'`.

figure.pyi has a stub for bivar_colorbar but no stub for multivar_colorbar, even though Figure.multivar_colorbar was added to figure.py. The stub file must also expose multivar_colorbar with its return type. Without this, type-checkers will report an unknown attribute.

colorbar.pyi adds BivarColorbar but not MultivarColorbar. MultivarColorbar is a public class (returned by Figure.multivar_colorbar) and requires a stub with at least: __init__, __getitem__, __len__, update_normals, remove, _set_colorbar_info.

pyplot.bivar_colorbar was added but pyplot.multivar_colorbar was not, even though Figure.multivar_colorbar was added. The two APIs should be symmetric. If the omission is intentional, add a comment explaining why and open a follow-up issue.
make_multivar_axes docstring contains the typo 'mulitvariate' (should be 'multivariate').

In the _bivar_colormap_kw_doc interpolation string, the ticklocations description has a double-quote typo: `{'auto', 'top', 'bottom''}` — extra trailing single-quote after 'bottom' before the closing brace.

BivarColorbar.__init__ does not accept **kwargs, yet Figure.bivar_colorbar forwards unrecognised keyword arguments after filtering only ['fraction', 'pad', 'shrink', 'anchor', 'panchor']. Arguments like 'orientation' or 'ticklocation' that survive the filter will cause a TypeError inside BivarColorbar.__init__. Either accept and document **kwargs in BivarColorbar, or expand the NON_COLORBAR_KEYS filter to include every argument consumed by make_bivar_axes/make_bivar_axes_gridspec.

test_bivar_colorbar_location is parametrized with use_gridspec=[True] only. The False path (make_bivar_axes instead of make_bivar_axes_gridspec) has no image-comparison regression coverage. Add use_gridspec=False to the parametrize list.

BivarColorbar._draw_all() accesses self.colorizer.norm.norms[0] and .norms[1] without checking that norm is a MultiNorm instance with at least 2 components. If a user attaches a plain Normalize, this raises AttributeError with no helpful message. Add an isinstance guard or raise a clear ValueError.

_scale_norm now checks isinstance(norm, colors.Norm) for the vmin/vmax conflict guard, but colors.Norm is the abstract base that includes MultiNorm. For the multivariate case, passing both norm=(n1,n2) and vmin=(...), vmax=(...) is intentional and valid. The ValueError should only apply to scalar norms (colors.Normalize), not MultiNorm. Limit the check to `isinstance(norm, colors.Normalize) and not isinstance(norm, colors.MultiNorm)`.

test_bivar_cbar_ticklocations has bare comparison expressions that are discarded: `cbar.ax.yaxis.get_label_position() == 'left'` and `cbar.ax.xaxis.get_label_position() == 'top'` are no-ops. They must be prefaced with `assert` to actually test anything.
Extra blank line after the _log assignment introduces a double blank line before the _docstring.interpd.register call. Not critical but inconsistent with surrounding style.
Closing parenthesis of BivarColorbar.__init__ signature is indented at 8 spaces while `def __init__` uses 4-space indent. The closing `):` should be at 4-space indent to match PEP 8 / repo style.

Docstring for bivar_colorbar has a formatting error: `for the`.pyplot.bivar_colorbar` function` — missing a space between 'the' and the backtick cross-reference. Should be `for the `.pyplot.bivar_colorbar` function`.

The if/else for `rasterized` and `lut` can be simplified to ternary expressions: `rasterized = n * m > self.n_rasterize` and `lut = lut_size if rasterized else None`. The current branching adds visual noise without clarity benefit.

New docstring entry `- (K, M, N): if coupled with a cmap that supports K scalars` is inconsistent in style with surrounding entries (no terminal period, vague phrasing). Suggested: `- (K, M, N): an image with K scalar channels. Requires a multivariate colormap accepting K channels; see *cmap*.`
